name: Plainstown
description: A walled village of timber and slate set against the eastern foothills. Its lanes still hold the smell of woodsmoke and turned earth — the troubles here are small ones, but every villager has begun looking west more often than they used to.

npcs.name: Lonny
dialogue: Fear the wild and avoid dark places until you are ready. For we will need you to one day confront the great worm.

4,5._note: shop roof — bridge sprite, not walkable

npcs.name: Brennan
dialogue: Welcome to the General Stores. Coin or barter, both are good with me.
Times are uneasy — folk buy more rope and lamp oil than they used to.
If you're heading west into the wilds, take twice what you think you need.
Safe travels, friend.

npcs.name: Brother Halen
dialogue: Peace upon you, traveller. The shrine is open to all who walk in faith.
We tend the lamp here as our forebears did, against the day the worm wakes.
If you carry wounds of body or spirit, lay them down a moment.
Walk in the light, friend.

npcs.name: Old Vesper
dialogue: Few find their way this far in. Sit, if you like — the stone is colder than the company.
I've been reading the marks on these walls for thirty years. They were old before my grandfather's grandfather.
The worm is in them. Coiled. Waiting. The wise thing is preparation, not panic.
Go well, and watch the shadows where the light bends.

npcs.name: Mara
dialogue: Oh — you startled me. We don't get many strangers walking right in.
My husband works the eastern fields. He says the soil sours a little more each season.
Stay if you like. The kettle's still warm.

npcs.name: Tomas
dialogue: Heard you came in from the road. Anything moving out there?
Children won't sleep some nights — they say a great worm circles the hills in their dreams.
Maybe it's just talk. Maybe it isn't.

npcs.name: Senna
dialogue: If you're a tracker, I'd pay coin for word of my brother — went north a fortnight ago.
He's a stubborn man. Carries a notched blade and a green cloak.
Just bring word. Alive or otherwise. I need to know.

name: Shanty Town
description: A clutter of fishing shacks and salvage-built docks pressed against the cliffs of the western shore. Half the boats no longer go out. The folk here were the first to see the dragon's shadow on the water — and the first whose dead refused to lie quiet.

npcs.name: Father Aldous
dialogue: Peace be upon you, wanderer.
In Terran's name, we mend body and soul.
Even the gravest wounds yield to Terran's power.
Walk always in the light.

npcs.name: Gruff
dialogue: Ah, customers! Welcome to Gruff's Armaments.
You won't find better quality anywhere else.
Every item here has been tested in the field.
Gold well spent is gold well earned!

npcs.name: Elder Sybil
dialogue: Welcome, travelers. I am the keeper of this town's history.
These lands were peaceful once, before the darkness came.
Be wary of the wilds -- not all creatures are friendly.
May fortune smile upon your journey.

npcs.name: Calla
dialogue: I lost my cat somewhere near the mountains...
Have you seen a tabby?

npcs.name: Old Pell
dialogue: Hush — close the door behind you, the wind off the dunes carries grit.
I've lived in Shanty Town all my life. Wasn't always this dry.
Used to be the river ran clean through here. Now we drink what the well coughs up.

npcs.name: Wynn
dialogue: Mind the floor — board's loose by the window.
If you're heading deeper, mind the gravesoil paths even more. Things move there at dusk.
The Elder will want a word with you before you go. She always does.

name: Seat of the Realm
description: The capital fortress, banner-hung and ringed with sun-rune walls. Queen Aelyse Veyron, last of the Sun-Priestess line, holds court here. The realm's archives, its mages, and its standing army all answer to this place — and all of them, lately, are stretched thin.

npcs.name: Archivist Cael
dialogue: I am Archivist Cael. Every charter, every chronicle, every binding-rite copied since the founding rests under this roof.
A thousand years past, the Conclave of Suns drew the dragon Hargorn beneath the western sea. Three Sealstones held the binding.
One stone is broken. One was stolen out of Plainstown in the night. The third still stands at the strait — and on its strength alone the realm now rests.
Read what we have, traveller. The Sun-Priests left answers we are too late to ask of them.

npcs.name: Court Mage Lirien
dialogue: Court Mage Lirien, at your service. I tend what is left of the wards over the strait.
Vorkhaal was one of us — a Conclave-brother turned heretic. He drinks the seal-magic to cheat his own death, and the wards thin to feed him.
Each season I feel the wardlines slip a finger's width westward. We do not have decades, traveller. We may not have years.
If your road takes you to the mountain coven, recover the binding-rite and end his work. Without it the Sun Sword is just a sword, and the dragon owns the coast.

npcs.name: Captain Halric
dialogue: Captain Halric of the Sun Guard. If you wear a sword in Her Majesty's hall, see that you can use it.
Half my company rides the western passes with Captain Roland. The other half stand the docks, watching for what crawls out of the burning surf.
Orcs that once kept to the deep west walk the foothills openly now. The wards' weakness has emboldened every wretched thing west of the river.
Banner of the Sun above us, blade in hand, and not one soldier over to spare. That is the realm in this hour.

npcs.name: Sister Mireille
dialogue: Light of the Sun upon you. I am Sister Mireille of the inner shrine.
Her Majesty Queen Aelyse Veyron is the last of the Sun-Priestess line. When she falls, the rite that crowns the realm dies with her.
There is a sword that drew Hargorn's blood once, long ago. It rests beneath the drowned Sea Shrine, waiting for hands that can carry it.
Pray for the Queen, traveller. She prays for us all.

npcs.name: Madala
dialogue: Welcome to the Crown General Stores — finest goods this side of the realm.
Provisions, rope, lanterns, parchment — if you can name it, I likely have it.
Royal coin is preferred. Other coin is accepted, with a small surcharge.
Walk on, and walk well.

npcs.name: Borric Ironhand
dialogue: Pick a blade — they're all sharper than your wits, no offence.
Steel from the northern smelters, finished here under Crown standard.
If a blade breaks in your hand within a moon, bring it back. I'll take it personally.
Mind the edges.

npcs.name: Sister Iona
dialogue: The light keeps watch over even the Crown City — the lamps here have not gone dark in a hundred years.
Speak your trouble, if you carry one. The shrine listens better than most courts.
We mend what can be mended. The rest, we carry together.
Go in peace, friend.
